basics.name: Kurayi Chawatama
basics.label: Molecular Biology and Genetics Undergraduate Student
basics.summary: Proudly Zimbabwean Molecular Biology and Genetics undergraduate at Uludağ University focused on cancer genomics, transcriptomics, and bioinformatics.

work.summary: Characterizing tumor microenvironment transcriptome variations in NASH and viral hepatocellular carcinomas for targeted therapy.
highlights: Proposed and secured a grant for cross-species scRNA-seq integration and differential analysis.
Validated findings in vitro using an FFA-induced HCC model.
Preliminary poster received the honor prize at a bioinformatics symposium.

work.summary: Worked with Nextflow pipelines for large-scale cancer genomics and multi-omics analysis.
highlights: Supported WES/WGS somatic variant calling and mutational signature analysis.
Processed ChIP-seq, Methyl-seq, and Bulk RNA-seq data at scale.

work.summary: Developed and validated a regression-based method for cancer cell plasticity scoring using CITE-seq data.
highlights: Decoded gene signatures associated with cancer cell plasticity in hepatocellular carcinoma.
Validated the signature in TCGA-LIHC with Cox regression analysis.

work.summary: Performed bioinformatics analyses in tumor immunology using bulk/single-cell RNA-seq, TCR-seq, and microarray data.
highlights: Assisted with lentiviral transduction, cell culture passaging, mouse xenograft modeling, and flow cytometry.

work.summary: Worked on the comprehensive analysis of GPRC5A promoter methylation across cancer types.
highlights: Presented a methyl-seq pilot study poster at a bioinformatics symposium.
Performed DNA isolation, PCR, and agarose gel electrophoresis.
